summary: The server-rendered discussion forms do not include any CSRF token or equivalent anti-CSRF validation. A malicious site can cause a logged-in course member to create or modify posts and comments, or delete their own accessible discussion content, using the victim's existing session.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('posts.create_post_submit', course_id=course['course_id']) }}">
  <label for="title">Title</label>
  <input id="title" name="title" required>
  <label for="body">Body</label>
  <textarea id="body" name="body" rows="8"></textarea>
  <button type="submit">Create Post</button>
evidence_snippets.reason: The post-creation form performs a state-changing request but contains no CSRF token or nonce.

evidence_snippets.code:
<form method="post" action="{{ url_for('posts.update_post_submit', course_id=course['course_id'], post_id=post['post_id']) }}">
  <label for="title">Title</label>
  <input id="title" name="title" value="{{ post['title'] }}" required>
  <label for="body">Body</label>
  <textarea id="body" name="body" rows="8">{{ post['body'] }}</textarea>
  <button type="submit">Update Post</button>
evidence_snippets.reason: The post-update form is forgeable because no anti-CSRF field is present.

evidence_snippets.code:
Add Comment

...
...
evidence_snippets.reason: The delete-post, create-comment, update-comment, and delete-comment forms all perform state-changing requests without any request authenticity token.
The handler accepts browser form POST requests using only cookie-based session state and does not verify any CSRF token.
The post update handler processes authenticated browser POST requests without validating request authenticity.
These state-changing discussion handlers rely solely on cookie-authenticated sessions and contain no CSRF validation.
These discussion mutation handlers accept authenticated browser POST requests without verifying a per-session CSRF token or equivalent defense.

root_cause: Server-rendered discussion endpoints rely on cookie-based authentication but do not require a request authenticity token or equivalent CSRF defense for state-changing form submissions.
repair_plan: Introduce server-side CSRF protection for all browser discussion workflows by generating a per-session token, embedding it in every state-changing post and comment form, and rejecting requests whose token is missing or invalid. Apply this consistently to create, update, and delete actions for both posts and comments.
